title: fix(lock): Fix LockError message when releasing a lock.
body:
Fix #3535 

### Pull Request check-list

_Please make sure to review and check all of these items:_

- [ ] Do tests and lints pass with this change?
- [ ] Do the CI tests pass with this change (enable it first in your forked repo and wait for the github action build to finish)?
- [ ] Is the new or changed code fully tested?
- [ ] Is a documentation update included (if this change modifies existing APIs, or introduces new ones)?
- [ ] Is there an example added to the examples folder (if applicable)?
- [ ] Was the change added to CHANGES file?

_NOTE: these things are not required to open a PR and can be done
afterwards / while the PR is open._

### Description of change

We can get expect result for the test code in #3535  with this implementation:
```
Thread 2: Lock error: Cannot release a lock that's no longer owned
```

Explore the source code to identify the files and functions to modify.

step_plan.summary: Fix LockError so the exception message is visible when printed by initializing the base Exception with the message (populating Exception.args), which also fixes message visibility for LockNotOwnedError.

steps.operation: Populate base Exception args in LockError constructor
steps.reason: LockError currently sets self.message and self.lock_name but does not initialize the base Exception with the message, so str(exception) (and Exception.__str__) yields an empty string. Calling super().__init__(message) (or setting self.args = (message,)) ensures the message is included in Exception.args and visible via str(exception). Retain existing attributes (self.message and self.lock_name) for backward compatibility.
steps.side_effects: Behavioral: Printed/logged exceptions (e.g., f"Lock error: {exc}") will now include the human-readable message such as "Cannot release a lock that's no longer owned". Backward compatibility: existing code that reads exception.message or exception.lock_name is unaffected. Tests: add or update a unit test to assert str(LockNotOwnedError(...)) contains the message to prevent regressions. Dependents: all code that raises or catches LockError/LockNotOwnedError continues to work; code that previously relied on Exception.args being empty (unlikely) might observe the populated args. No API signature changes; consumers that inspect Exception.args will now see the message. CI: run unit tests and linters to ensure no other code expects the previous behavior.
